title: Getting Started
text: Install r-universe: r-universe build includes Rust DuckDB backend macOS Linux, powers streaming point pipeline freestile_query(). can also install GitHub devtools::install_github("walkerke/freestiler"). Python, see Python Setup article.
code: install.packages(   "freestiler",   repos = c("https://walkerke.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") )

title: Getting Started
text: main function freestile(). Let’s tile North Carolina counties dataset ships sf: ’s useful verifying installation, let’s try something interesting.
code: library(sf) library(freestiler)  nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  freestile(nc, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") Creating MVT tiles (zoom 0-14) for 100 features across 1 layer...   Tiling layer 'counties' (zoom 0-14)... Created nc_counties.pmtiles (65.2 KB)

title: Getting Started
text: Let’s tile 242,000 US block groups tigris package. takes 20 seconds machine produces tileset can zoom national level individual neighborhoods:
code: library(tigris) options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)  bgs <- block_groups(cb = TRUE)  freestile(   bgs,   "us_bgs.pmtiles",   layer_name = "bgs",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 12 )

title: Getting Started
text: quickest way view tileset view_tiles(), starts local HTTP server creates interactive map one step: auto-detects layer name geometry type PMTiles metadata, works box polygon, line, point data. control styling layers, use serve_tiles() start server separately build map mapgl. See Mapping mapgl article full walkthrough workflow. built-server handles CORS HTTP range requests automatically. tilesets larger ~1 GB, ’ll get better performance external server:
code: view_tiles("us_bgs.pmtiles") npx http-server /path/to/tiles -p 8082 --cors -c-1

title: Getting Started
text: default tile format MVT, broad viewer compatibility including MapLibre GL JS Mapbox GL JS. potentially smaller files polygon-heavy data, can use experimental MLT format:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_mlt.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties", tile_format = "mlt")

title: Getting Started
text: Use min_zoom max_zoom set zoom range tileset:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_z4_10.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 10 )

title: Getting Started
text: large datasets, drop_rate provides exponential feature thinning lower zoom levels. Points thinned using spatial ordering maintain even coverage; polygons lines thinned area. base_zoom parameter controls zoom level features kept:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_dropping.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   drop_rate = 2.5,   base_zoom = 10 )

title: Getting Started
text: can tile spatial files disk without loading R first. useful large GeoParquet files formats ’d rather pull memory. Non-WGS84 files automatically reprojected:
code: # GeoParquet freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  # GeoPackage, Shapefile, or other formats via DuckDB freestile_file("counties.gpkg", "counties.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb")

title: Getting Started
text: data already lives DuckDB, can run SQL query pipe results directly tiling engine. lets filter, join, transform data SQL tiling: large point datasets, set streaming = "always" use streaming pipeline, avoids loading full query result memory: recent run, streamed 146 million US job points DuckDB 2.3 GB PMTiles archive 12 minutes.
code: freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM ST_Read('counties.shp') WHERE pop > 50000",   "large_counties.pmtiles" ) freestile_query(   query = "SELECT naics, state, ST_Point(lon, lat) AS geometry FROM jobs_dots",   output = "us_jobs_dots.pmtiles",   db_path = db_path,   layer_name = "jobs",   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 14,   base_zoom = 14,   drop_rate = 2.5,   source_crs = "EPSG:4326",   streaming = "always",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Getting Started
text: Pass named list create multi-layer tilesets. Use freestile_layer() want per-layer zoom control:
code: pts <- st_centroid(nc)  freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     centroids = freestile_layer(pts, min_zoom = 6, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" )

title: Getting Started
text: point layers, cluster_distance merges nearby points clusters point_count attribute:
code: freestile(pts, "nc_clustered.pmtiles",   layer_name = "centroids",   cluster_distance = 50,   cluster_maxzoom = 8 )

title: Getting Started
text: coalesce parameter merges features identical attributes within tile. Lines sharing endpoints joined, polygons grouped MultiPolygons:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_coalesced.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   coalesce = TRUE )

title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: freestile_h3() uses DuckDB H3 community extension binning. first call, DuckDB downloads extension automatically (INSTALL h3 community), need network access first time. R, install DBI duckdb packages (mapgl viewing): Python, install h3 extra, pulls duckdb package:
code: install.packages(c("DBI", "duckdb", "mapgl")) pip install 'freestiler[h3]'

title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: Let’s tile US Wind Turbine Database, roughly 75,000 turbines USGS tracks across country. API returns one row per turbine longitude, latitude, turbine’s nameplate capacity kilowatts. call Python takes GeoDataFrame: freestile_h3() writes one MVT source-layer per H3 resolution (h3_r02, h3_r03, ) plus points source-layer raw data. default fade = FALSE, layers disjoint zoom windows, map swaps hexagon resolutions cleanly zoom replaces hexagons individual turbines base_zoom.
code: library(freestiler) library(sf)  url <- "https://energy.usgs.gov/api/uswtdb/v1/turbines?select=t_state,p_name,t_manu,t_cap,xlong,ylat" turbines <- jsonlite::fromJSON(url) turbines$capacity_mw <- turbines$t_cap / 1000   # t_cap is in kilowatts  turbines <- st_as_sf(turbines, coords = c("xlong", "ylat"), crs = 4326)  freestile_h3(   turbines,   "turbines.pmtiles",   min_zoom = 2,   max_zoom = 12,   base_zoom = 10 ) import pandas as pd import geopandas as gpd from freestiler import freestile_h3  url = "https://energy.usgs.gov/api/uswtdb/v1/turbines?select=t_state,p_name,t_manu,t_cap,xlong,ylat" turbines = pd.read_json(url) turbines["capacity_mw"] = turbines["t_cap"] / 1000   # t_cap is in kilowatts  turbines = gpd.GeoDataFrame(     turbines,     geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(turbines.xlong, turbines.ylat),     crs="EPSG:4326", )  freestile_h3(turbines, "turbines.pmtiles", min_zoom=2, max_zoom=12, base_zoom=10)

title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: agg argument controls summary properties hexagon carries. simplest case counts points hex; agg = "count" default. richer summaries, pass named vector SQL aggregations: gives hexagon turbine count (n), total installed capacity megawatts (total_mw), average turbine size (avg_mw), climbed steadily turbine technology grown. ’d rather write SQL, pass named list c(fn, column) pairs instead: Supported function names: count, sum, mean (alias avg), min, max, median. Python, agg dictionary. Map names SQL strings, (fn, column) tuples:
code: freestile_h3(turbines, "turbines.pmtiles", agg = "count") freestile_h3(   turbines, "turbines.pmtiles",   agg = c(     n        = "COUNT(*)",     total_mw = "SUM(capacity_mw)",     avg_mw   = "AVG(capacity_mw)"   ) ) freestile_h3(   turbines, "turbines.pmtiles",   agg = list(     n        = c("count", "*"),     total_mw = c("sum",  "capacity_mw"),     avg_mw   = c("mean", "capacity_mw")   ) ) freestile_h3(     turbines, "turbines.pmtiles",     agg={"n": "COUNT(*)", "total_mw": "SUM(capacity_mw)"}, )  freestile_h3(     turbines, "turbines.pmtiles",     agg={"n": ("count", "*"), "total_mw": ("sum", "capacity_mw")}, )

title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: view_h3_tiles() reads archive metadata, finds h3_r* layers points layer, builds mapgl map one shared color scale across hex resolutions. accepts quick-look default ramp first pass; finished map, pass explicit stops:  default scale (stops = NULL) spans 1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000 viridis palette. ’s fine first look, ’ll usually want set stops data’s range. view_h3_tiles() R helper. Python, serve archive static file server supports byte-range requests (built-http.server ), style mapgl MapLibre GL JS. work languages, Positron IDE lets tile Python map R session.
code: view_h3_tiles(   "turbines.pmtiles",   agg_column = "n",   stops = list(     values = c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000),     colors = viridisLite::viridis(5)   ) )

title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: points already live DuckDB, file can read (Parquet, CSV, GeoPackage, Shapefile), skip -memory roundtrip pass SQL directly. Save turbine coordinates file , let DuckDB build geometry capacity column columns: Python call , dictionary agg: read_csv_auto() read_parquet() built DuckDB, work R Python. (Reading remote files HTTP needs DuckDB’s httpfs json extensions, R duckdb package always bundle, download local file first.) Multi-statement SQL works : setup statements (CREATE VIEW, LOAD, ) run first, final SELECT input. keep utility-scale turbines 2 MW: Pass source_crs whenever SQL returns non-WGS84 geometry. omit , freestile_h3() assumes EPSG:4326 warns .
code: write.csv(   jsonlite::fromJSON(     "https://energy.usgs.gov/api/uswtdb/v1/turbines?select=xlong,ylat,t_cap"   ),   "turbines.csv", row.names = FALSE, na = "" )  freestile_h3(   "SELECT ST_Point(xlong, ylat) AS geometry, t_cap / 1000 AS capacity_mw      FROM read_csv_auto('turbines.csv')",   "turbines.pmtiles",   agg = c(n = "COUNT(*)", total_mw = "SUM(capacity_mw)"),   source_crs = "EPSG:4326" ) import pandas as pd  pd.read_json(     "https://energy.usgs.gov/api/uswtdb/v1/turbines?select=xlong,ylat,t_cap" ).to_csv("turbines.csv", index=False)  freestile_h3(     """SELECT ST_Point(xlong, ylat) AS geometry, t_cap / 1000 AS capacity_mw          FROM read_csv_auto('turbines.csv')""",     "turbines.pmtiles",     agg={"n": "COUNT(*)", "total_mw": "SUM(capacity_mw)"},     source_crs="EPSG:4326", ) freestile_h3(   paste(     "CREATE TEMP VIEW big AS",     "  SELECT * FROM read_csv_auto('turbines.csv') WHERE t_cap > 2000;",     "SELECT ST_Point(xlong, ylat) AS geometry, t_cap / 1000 AS capacity_mw FROM big"   ),   "big_turbines.pmtiles",   source_crs = "EPSG:4326" )

title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: default, hexagon resolutions swap cleanly zoom boundaries. blend transitions instead, coarser hexes fade finer ones fade , set fade = TRUE:  fade = TRUE, adjacent hex layers overlap fade_overlap zoom levels. view_h3_tiles() detects overlap gives layer trapezoidal fill_opacity envelope renderer cross-fades resolutions. Use larger fade_overlap (say 2) slower, diffuse blend, default 1 tighter handoff.
code: freestile_h3(   turbines, "turbines_fade.pmtiles",   agg = "count",   min_zoom = 2, max_zoom = 12, base_zoom = 10,   fade = TRUE         # default fade_overlap = 1 ) view_h3_tiles("turbines_fade.pmtiles", agg_column = "count", palette = "mako")

title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: MVT source-layer named "<hex_layer_prefix>_r<resolution>". default prefix "h3", ’ll see layer ids like h3_r02, h3_r03, h3_r07. raw-points layer defaults "points". can change either:
code: freestile_h3(   turbines, "turbines.pmtiles",   hex_layer_prefix = "wind",   # produces "wind_r02", "wind_r03", ...   point_layer_name = "turbines" )

what: Customizing the zoom to H3 resolution mapping
title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: defaults pair tile zoom H3 resolution whose hexagon edge length roughly matches tile pixel zoom. Override mapping h3_resolutions: override accepts: NULL use built-defaults. unnamed integer vector one entry per hex zoom (length(min_zoom:(base_zoom - 1))), mapped positionally. sparse named integer vector keyed zoom number, defaults filling gaps. resolutions must integers 0:15. resolution appearing non-contiguous zoom runs rejected, since layer names collide h3_rNN. Python, h3_resolutions takes list positional form dict keyed zoom sparse form.
code: # res 4 at zoom 0-3, res 6 at zoom 4-6, then points at 7+ freestile_h3(   turbines, "turbines.pmtiles",   min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10, base_zoom = 7,   h3_resolutions = c(4, 4, 4, 4, 6, 6, 6) )

what: Building a production map with mapgl
title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: view_h3_tiles() quick preview. map ’d actually ship, build directly mapgl, gives full control color scale, fade envelope, tooltips. pattern read layer list archive metadata, add one fill layer per H3 resolution, add circle layer points. First build fade archive carries columns want show: style . metadata lists every layer archive, pull hex layers purrr::keep(), sort coarse fine, fold fill layer onto map one purrr::reduce(). Every hex layer shares one color scale gets zoom-keyed fill_opacity envelope fades back across window, overlapping resolutions cross-fade:  Zoom past base_zoom hexes give way individual turbines. Click one popup brace template fills card straight point’s columns: project name bold, manufacturer, turbine’s capacity, state. tooltip works way hover, accept several {column} references . Wrap numeric column number_format() want control reads.
code:
freestile_h3( turbines, "turbines_fade.pmtiles", agg = c(n = "COUNT(*)", total_mw = "SUM(capacity_mw)"), min_zoom = 2, max_zoom = 12, base_zoom = 10, fade = TRUE ) library(mapgl) library(purrr) library(stringr) serve_tiles(".", port = 8080) meta <- pmtiles_metadata("turbines_fade.pmtiles") # pull the hex layers out of the metadata, ordered coarse to fine hex_layers <- meta$metadata$vector_layers |> keep(\(layer) str_starts(layer$id, "h3_r")) |> map(\(layer) list(id = layer$id, minzoom = layer$minzoom, maxzoom = layer$maxzoom)) hex_layers <- hex_layers[order(map_dbl(hex_layers, "minzoom"))] points_minzoom <- meta$metadata$vector_layers |> keep(\(layer) layer$id == "points") |> pluck(1, "minzoom") # one shared color scale across every resolution fill <- interpolate( column = "n", values = c(1, 10, 100, 1000), stops = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(4, "Purples") ) base_map <- maplibre( bounds = c(meta$min_longitude, meta$min_latitude, meta$max_longitude, meta$max_latitude) ) |> add_pmtiles_source(id = "turbines", url = "http://localhost:8080/turbines_fade.pmtiles") # fade each resolution in and back out across its zoom window add_hex_layer <- function(map, layer) { add_fill_layer( map, id = paste0("fill-", layer$id), source = "turbines", source_layer = layer$id, fill_color = fill, fill_opacity = interpolate( property = "zoom", values = c(layer$minzoom, layer$minzoom + 1, layer$maxzoom, layer$maxzoom + 1), stops = c(0, 0.85, 0.85, 0) ), fill_outline_color = "rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25)", min_zoom = layer$minzoom, max_zoom = layer$maxzoom + 1, # MapLibre maxzoom is exclusive tooltip = "{n} turbines", # brace template (mapgl >= 0.5.0) tooltip_style = tooltip_style("dark") ) } reduce(hex_layers, add_hex_layer, .init = base_map) |> add_circle_layer( id = "turbine-points", source = "turbines", source_layer = "points", circle_color = "#3b1f6b", circle_radius = 4, circle_stroke_color = "#ffffff", circle_stroke_width = 0.5, circle_opacity = 0.9, min_zoom = points_minzoom, popup = "{p_name}
{t_manu}, {capacity_mw} MW
{t_state}", popup_style = popup_style("light") )

title: Dynamic hexagonal binning with H3
text: Points . Polygon line aggregations (centroids h3_polygon_to_cells) may come future release. MULTIPOINT handled. Cast POINT first sf::st_cast(x, "POINT") R, gdf.explode() Python, ST_Centroid SQL. view_h3_tiles() R helper. Python users serve archive style mapgl MapLibre GL JS.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Vector tiles divide geographic data grid square tiles multiple zoom levels. Unlike raster tiles (pre-rendered images), vector tiles store actual geometry attribute data, client can style, interact , dynamically render data. standard container vector tilesets PMTiles - single-file archive can serve static file host.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT) dominant vector tile encoding since Mapbox introduced 2014. uses Protocol Buffers serialize geometry attributes, ’s supported every major mapping library: MapLibre GL, Mapbox GL, Leaflet, deck.gl, . need maximum compatibility, MVT safe choice.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: MVT stores data row--row (one feature time), MLT organizes data column--column - x-coordinates together, y-coordinates together, values given attribute together. columnar layout opens compression techniques aren’t available MVT’s row-oriented format: delta encoding coordinates, run-length encoding repeated values, dictionary encoding string columns low cardinality. practical result smaller tiles, especially polygon-heavy datasets: Point-datasets see minimal savings coordinate data dominates compresses similarly formats.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Use MLT ’re working R mapgl viewing browser MapLibre GL JS 5.21+, want smaller file sizes polygon line data. Use MVT (default) widest viewer compatibility - works MapLibre GL JS, Mapbox GL JS (now supports PMTiles natively v3.21), deck.gl, . can switch formats single argument:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_mlt.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties", tile_format = "mlt")

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: MLT announced January 2026, library support growing: Encoding: freestiler (Rust, R + Python), mlt-core reference encoder (Java/C++) Decoding: MapLibre GL JS (experimental, v5.21+), mlt-core decoder Viewing: MapLibre GL JS, mapgl R

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: First, pull block group geometries income data tidycensus: actual tiling takes minutes produces ~250 MB tileset. output include:
code: library(tidycensus) library(freestiler) options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)  # Pulls data state-by-state, so will take a few minutes if  # geometries are not pre-cached bgs <- get_acs(   geography = "block group",   variables = "B19013_001",  # median household income   state = c(state.abb, "DC"),   year = 2024,   geometry = TRUE )  freestile(   bgs,   "us_income.pmtiles",   layer_name = "income",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 12 ) Created us_income.pmtiles (252.6 MB) View with: view_tiles("us_income.pmtiles")

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: fast look tileset, use view_tiles():  auto-detects layer name, geometry type, bounds PMTiles metadata, starts local server, opens interactive map. works polygon, line, point data without extra configuration. FYI, specific example - given USA’s bounding box, ’ll need pan continental US view .
code: view_tiles("us_income.pmtiles")

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: control styling, use serve_tiles() start local server, build map mapgl. PMTiles require server supports CORS HTTP range requests — serve_tiles() handles . Now add tileset source style :  Key components: add_pmtiles_source() registers PMTiles file vector tile source. promote_id parameter tells MapLibre property use feature ID (needed hover/click interactivity). source_layer must match layer_name used freestile(). interpolate() creates data-driven color ramp. MapLibre evaluates per-feature GPU, ’s fast even 242K polygons. hover_options highlights features mouseover. requires promote_id set source. tooltip uses concat() number_format() build formatted hover text feature properties.
code: library(mapgl)  serve_tiles("us_income.pmtiles") maplibre(   style = openfreemap_style("positron"),   bounds = c(-125, 24, -66, 50),  ) |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "income-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/us_income.pmtiles",     promote_id = "GEOID"   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "income-fill",     source = "income-src",     source_layer = "income",     fill_color = interpolate(       column = "estimate",       values = c(20000, 50000, 100000, 200000),       stops = c("#d73027", "#fee08b", "#91cf60", "#1a9850"),       na_color = "#cccccc"     ),     fill_opacity = 0.7,     hover_options = list(       fill_opacity = 1     ),     tooltip = concat(       "Median income: $",       number_format(get_column("estimate"), locale = "en")     )   )

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: line data, use add_line_layer(): point data, use add_circle_layer():
code: maplibre() |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "roads-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/roads.pmtiles"   ) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "roads",     source = "roads-src",     source_layer = "roads",     line_color = "#4a90d9",     line_width = 1.5   ) maplibre() |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "pts-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/airports.pmtiles"   ) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "airports",     source = "pts-src",     source_layer = "airports",     circle_color = "orange",     circle_radius = 5,     circle_stroke_color = "white",     circle_stroke_width = 1   )

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: tileset multiple layers (created named list freestile()), add layer separately — share source:
code: maplibre() |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "nc-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/nc_layers.pmtiles"   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "counties",     source = "nc-src",     source_layer = "counties",     fill_color = "navy",     fill_opacity = 0.3   ) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "centroids",     source = "nc-src",     source_layer = "centroids",     circle_color = "red",     circle_radius = 4   )

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: freestiler defaults Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT), broadest viewer compatibility across MapLibre GL JS Mapbox GL JS. potentially smaller files polygon-heavy data, can use experimental MapLibre Tiles (MLT) format:
code: freestile(bgs, "us_income_mlt.pmtiles",   layer_name = "income",   tile_format = "mlt" )

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: built-serve_tiles() server works well files 1 GB. larger tilesets, use external server better concurrency. terminal, assuming Node’s http-server installed: point add_pmtiles_source() URL http://localhost:8082/... instead. Stop external server Ctrl+C terminal ’re done. ’ll see better performance method built-server datasets 1GB well, like block groups dataset example.
code: npx http-server /path/to/tiles -p 8082 --cors -c-1

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: ’re done viewing built-server, stop :
code: stop_server()

title: Python Setup
text: Install PyPI: Published wheels include full feature set: GeoPandas input, direct file tiling, DuckDB-backed file input, SQL query support. Supported wheel targets Python 3.9 3.14.
code: pip install freestiler

title: Python Setup
text: need Rust toolchain wheel isn’t available platform want editable local build:
code: git clone https://github.com/walkerke/freestiler.git cd freestiler/python python3 -m venv .venv source .venv/bin/activate pip install maturin python3 -m maturin develop --release

title: Python Setup
text: Python API mirrors R closely. ’s equivalent North Carolina example Getting Started article: Multi-layer tilesets use dictionary instead R’s named list: File input DuckDB queries work way:
code: import geopandas as gpd from freestiler import freestile  url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r-spatial/sf/main/inst/gpkg/nc.gpkg" gdf = gpd.read_file(url)  freestile(gdf, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name="counties") from freestiler import freestile, freestile_layer  centroids = gdf.copy() centroids.geometry = gdf.geometry.centroid  freestile(     {         "counties": freestile_layer(gdf, min_zoom=0, max_zoom=10),         "centroids": freestile_layer(centroids, min_zoom=6, max_zoom=14),     },     "nc_layers.pmtiles" ) from freestiler import freestile_file, freestile_query  freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  freestile_query(     query="SELECT * FROM read_parquet('blocks.parquet') WHERE state = 'NC'",     output="nc_blocks.pmtiles",     layer_name="blocks" )

title: Python Setup
text: freestile(gdf, ...) convenient Python entry point, preprocessing GeoPandas Rust starts tiling. large datasets, freestile_file() freestile_query() usually faster path. practice, expensive part GeoDataFrame path often reprojection WGS84 plus geometry serialization Rust tiler takes . data already disk DuckDB, prefer paths serious workloads.

title: Python Setup
text: ’d recommend creating tiles tile_format="mvt" Python-facing work now. Python viewer stacks still catching MLT support, MVT works everywhere. One important caveat: Python’s built-http.server support byte-range requests, won’t work PMTiles server. ’ll want use real static file server instead: can view PMTiles file (MLT MVT) browser MapLibre GL JS 5.17+ PMTiles protocol. ’re also R user, mapgl package reliable local viewing path right now. recommendation Python users: try Positron IDE, supports Python R simultaneously. ’ll able tiling Python easily move R mapping mapgl package. Read mapping vignette learn .
code: npx http-server /path/to/tiles -p 8082 --cors -c-1

code: @Manual{,   title = {freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data},   author = {Kyle Walker},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://walker-data.com/freestiler/}, }

title: freestiler
text: freestiler creates PMTiles vector tilesets R Python. Give sf object, file disk, DuckDB SQL query, writes single .pmtiles file can serve anywhere. tiling engine written Rust runs -process, ’s nothing else install.

title: freestiler
text: freestiler available CRAN: full feature set powered Rust DuckDB (MacOS Linux ), install r-universe: install GitHub:
code: install.packages("freestiler") install.packages(   "freestiler",   repos = c("https://walkerke.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("walkerke/freestiler")

title: freestiler
text: Published PyPI wheels currently target Python 3.9 3.14. See Python Setup article details.
code: pip install freestiler

title: freestiler
text: main function freestile(). Let’s tile North Carolina counties dataset ships sf: ’s useful checking installation, API handles much bigger data. tile 242,000 US block groups tigris:
code: library(sf) library(freestiler)  nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  freestile(nc, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") library(tigris) options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)  bgs <- block_groups(cb = TRUE)  freestile(   bgs,   "us_bgs.pmtiles",   layer_name = "bgs",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 12 )

title: freestiler
text: quickest way view tileset view_tiles(), starts local server opens interactive map: control, use serve_tiles() start local server build map mapgl: built-server handles CORS range requests automatically. tilesets larger ~1 GB, use external server like npx http-server /path --cors -c-1 better performance. See Mapping mapgl article full walkthrough.
code: view_tiles("us_bgs.pmtiles") library(mapgl)  serve_tiles("us_bgs.pmtiles")  maplibre(hash = TRUE) |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "bgs-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/us_bgs.pmtiles",     promote_id = "GEOID"   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "bgs-fill",     source = "bgs-src",     source_layer = "bgs",     fill_color = "navy",     fill_opacity = 0.5,     hover_options = list(       fill_color = "#ffffcc",       fill_opacity = 0.9     )   )

title: freestiler
text: data lives DuckDB, freestile_query() lets filter, join, transform SQL tiling: large point datasets, streaming pipeline avoids loading full result memory. recent run, freestile_query() streamed 146 million US job points DuckDB 2.3 GB PMTiles archive 12 minutes:
code: freestile_query(   query = "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('blocks.parquet') WHERE state = 'NC'",   output = "nc_blocks.pmtiles",   layer_name = "blocks" ) freestile_query(   query = "SELECT naics, state, ST_Point(lon, lat) AS geometry FROM jobs_dots",   output = "us_jobs_dots.pmtiles",   db_path = db_path,   layer_name = "jobs",   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 14,   base_zoom = 14,   drop_rate = 2.5,   source_crs = "EPSG:4326",   streaming = "always",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: freestiler
text: can tile spatial files without loading R first:
code: # GeoParquet freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  # GeoPackage, Shapefile, or other formats via DuckDB freestile_file("counties.gpkg", "counties.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb")

title: freestiler
text: dense point datasets, freestile_h3() aggregates points H3 hexagons zoom-appropriate resolutions: low zooms show coarse hexes summarizing whole regions, intermediate zooms show progressively finer hexes, base_zoom reveal underlying points. Aggregation rules user-defined SQL (COUNT(*), SUM(pop), AVG(value), …). Pass fade = TRUE cross-fade resolutions instead swapping cleanly. Requires DuckDB H3 community extension; see Hexagonal binning H3 article.
code: freestile_h3(   pts,   "wind.pmtiles",   agg = c(n = "COUNT(*)", total_mw = "SUM(capacity_mw)"),   min_zoom = 2, max_zoom = 12, base_zoom = 10 )  view_h3_tiles("wind.pmtiles", agg_column = "n")

title: freestiler
code: pts <- st_centroid(nc)  freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     centroids = freestile_layer(pts, min_zoom = 6, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" )

title: freestiler
text: freestiler defaults Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT), widely-supported protobuf format works MapLibre GL JS Mapbox GL JS. experimental MapLibre Tiles (MLT) format also available via tile_format = "mlt" can produce smaller files polygon line data.

title: freestiler
text: Getting Started - full tutorial Mapping mapgl - viewing styling tiles mapgl MapLibre Tiles (MLT) - MLT vs MVT use Python Setup - Python installation usage

what: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: Creates PMTiles archive containing vector tiles one sf data frames. Supports Mapbox Vector Tile (MVT) MapLibre Tile (MLT) formats, multi-layer output, feature dropping, point clustering, feature coalescing.

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
code: freestile(   input,   output,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   generate_ids = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: input sf data frame, named list sf/freestile_layer objects multi-layer output. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. used single-layer input. tile_format Character. Tile encoding format: "mvt" (default) Mapbox Vector Tiles "mlt" MapLibre Tiles. min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present (dropping). NULL (default) uses layer's max_zoom. drop-rate curve also computed relative base_zoom, lowering produces gentler thinning low zooms. Inspired tippecanoe's -B / --base-zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate feature thinning (e.g. 2.5). zoom level base_zoom, features retained rate 1/drop_rate^(base_zoom - zoom). Points thinned using spatial ordering; polygons/lines thinned area. NULL (default) disables drop-rate thinning. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance point clustering. Points within radius merged cluster features point_count attribute. NULL (default) disables clustering. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Maximum zoom level clustering. zoom, individual points shown. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes within tile (default FALSE). Lines sharing endpoints merged; polygons grouped MultiPolygons. simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid zoom level (default TRUE). provides zoom-adaptive simplification prevents slivers adjacent polygons. generate_ids Logical. Whether assign sequential feature IDs (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output file (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress messages (default FALSE).

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: Input data coordinate reference system (CRS) automatically reprojected WGS84 (EPSG:4326) tiling.

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  # Single layer freestile(nc, "nc.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties")  # Multi-layer pts <- st_centroid(nc) freestile(   list(counties = nc, centroids = pts),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" )  # With dropping and coalescing freestile(nc, "nc_drop.pmtiles", drop_rate = 2.5, coalesce = TRUE)  # With point clustering freestile(pts, "pts.pmtiles", cluster_distance = 50, cluster_maxzoom = 8) } # }

what: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: Reads GeoParquet, GeoPackage, Shapefile, spatial file directly tiling engine. Input data coordinate reference system automatically reprojected WGS84 (EPSG:4326) tiling.

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
code: freestile_file(   input,   output,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   engine = "geoparquet" )

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: input Character. Path input spatial file. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. tile_format Character. "mvt" (default) "mlt". min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present. NULL (default) uses max_zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate. NULL (default) disables. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance clustering. NULL disables. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Max zoom clustering. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes (default FALSE). simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress (default FALSE). engine Character. Backend engine: "geoparquet" (default, GeoParquet files) "duckdb" (file format DuckDB supports).

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: GeoParquet engine requires compilation FREESTILER_GEOPARQUET=true. DuckDB engine uses Rust DuckDB backend included build (enabled default native builds), falls back R duckdb package. Control backend selection options(freestiler.duckdb_backend = "auto"|"rust"|"r").

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ freestile_file("data.parquet", "output.pmtiles") freestile_file("data.gpkg", "output.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb") } # }

what: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
title: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
text: Aggregates points H3 hexagons zoom-appropriate resolutions writes PMTiles archive low zooms show coarse hexagons, intermediate zooms show progressively finer hexagons, zooms base_zoom show individual points. Aggregations (count, sum, mean, etc.) computed DuckDB via H3 community extension; function assembles per-resolution hex layers raw-point layer via freestile().

title: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
code: freestile_h3(   input,   output,   agg = "count",   hex_layer_prefix = "h3",   point_layer_name = "points",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   h3_resolutions = NULL,   source_crs = NULL,   db_path = NULL,   tile_format = "mvt",   fade = FALSE,   fade_overlap = 1L,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
text: input sf data frame POINT geometry, character SQL query returns geometry column executed DuckDB. (DuckDB's spatial functions ST_Read() read_parquet() available.) output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. agg Aggregation specification: "count" (default): single count = COUNT(*) property. Named character vector SQL aggregation expressions, e.g. c(n = "COUNT(*)", avg_pop = "AVG(pop)", total = "SUM(pop)"). Named list prop_name = c(fn, column) callers want write SQL, e.g. list(n = c("count", "*"), avg_pop = c("mean", "pop")). Supported fn values: "count", "sum", "mean"/"avg", "min", "max", "median". hex_layer_prefix Character. Prefix per-resolution hex MVT layer names. Default "h3" produces "h3_r01", "h3_r02", etc. point_layer_name Character. MVT layer name raw points (default "points"). min_zoom, max_zoom Integer. Global zoom range (default 0–14). base_zoom Integer NULL. Zoom level raw points take hex aggregations. Default NULL resolves max_zoom - 2L, clamped min_zoom <= base_zoom <= max_zoom. base_zoom == min_zoom, hex layers created. h3_resolutions Optional override zoom -> H3 resolution mapping. Accepts NULL (use built-defaults), unnamed integer vector length(min_zoom:(base_zoom - 1L)) entries mapped positionally, named integer vector names parse integer zoom levels (sparse overrides; defaults fill rest). resolutions must integers 0:15. resolution appearing non-contiguous zoom runs (e.g. zooms 4–5 8) rejected. source_crs Character NULL. CRS geometry returned SQL input, e.g. "EPSG:4326" (default sf input, since sf auto-transformed WGS84) "EPSG:3857". Ignored sf input. SQL input, NULL, warning emitted geometry assumed "EPSG:4326". db_path Character NULL. Path existing DuckDB database file, NULL/"" (default) -memory database. tile_format Character. "mvt" (default) "mlt". fade Logical. TRUE, adjacent hex layer zoom windows overlap fade_overlap zooms view_h3_tiles() can cross-fade resolutions. Default FALSE (disjoint windows, clean zoom breaks). fade_overlap Integer. Zooms overlap side (used fade = TRUE, default 1L). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output file (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Suppress progress messages (default FALSE).

title: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
text: distinct H3 resolution becomes MVT source-layer (named "<hex_layer_prefix>_r<resolution>", e.g. "h3_r05"); raw points emitted separate source-layer (point_layer_name). default fade = FALSE, per-layer zoom windows disjoint rendered map swaps resolutions cleanly. fade = TRUE, adjacent windows overlap fade_overlap zooms companion view_h3_tiles() helper can cross-fade resolutions. DuckDB H3 community extension required. sf input data written DuckDB via temporary Parquet (dbWriteTable) roundtrip; character SQL input, user query wrapped temporary view inside DuckDB.

title: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
text: raw-point layer encoded without thinning: every input point written every tile contains base_zoom (base_zoom - fade_overlap fade = TRUE). large inputs tiles first point zoom can get heavy. Raising base_zoom defers points higher zooms, tile covers less area holds fewer points. Per-layer feature dropping points layer roadmap.

title: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
text: Hexagons cross antimeridian split +/-180 degrees render correctly sides dateline instead world-spanning slivers. rare cells contain pole receive split render approximately (polygon edge follows cell boundary vertices, polar cap filled).

title: Create vector tiles with dynamic H3 hexagonal binning — freestile_h3
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) pts <- st_as_sf(data.frame(   x = runif(50000, -100, -80),   y = runif(50000, 30, 45),   w = rnorm(50000, 100, 10) ), coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326)  freestile_h3(pts, "wind.pmtiles",   agg = c(n = "COUNT(*)", avg_w = "AVG(w)"),   min_zoom = 2, max_zoom = 12, base_zoom = 10)  view_h3_tiles("wind.pmtiles", agg_column = "n")  # Cross-fade between resolutions freestile_h3(pts, "wind_fade.pmtiles",   agg = "count",   min_zoom = 2, max_zoom = 12, base_zoom = 10,   fade = TRUE) } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf")) roads <- st_read("roads.shp")  freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     roads = freestile_layer(roads, min_zoom = 8, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "layers.pmtiles" ) } # }

what: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: Executes SQL query via DuckDB's spatial extension pipes results tiling engine. Uses Rust DuckDB backend included build (enabled default native builds), falls back R duckdb package. Control backend selection options(freestiler.duckdb_backend = "auto"|"rust"|"r").

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
code: freestile_query(   query,   output,   db_path = NULL,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   source_crs = NULL,   streaming = "auto" )

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: query Character. SQL query returns geometry column. DuckDB spatial functions like ST_Read() read_parquet() available. Multi-statement SQL supported: setup statements (e.g., LOAD h3;) executed first, final SELECT used tiling. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. db_path Character. Path DuckDB database file, NULL (default) -memory database. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. tile_format Character. "mvt" (default) "mlt". min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present. NULL (default) uses max_zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate. NULL (default) disables. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance clustering. NULL disables. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Max zoom clustering. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes (default FALSE). simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress (default FALSE). source_crs Character NULL. CRS geometry returned query, example "EPSG:4326" "EPSG:4267". Used R duckdb fallback; ignored Rust DuckDB backend. streaming Character. DuckDB query execution mode: "auto" (default) enables streaming point pipeline large queries, "always" forces , "never" uses existing -memory path.

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: using R fallback, source_crs must supplied explicitly query result can interpreted reprojected correctly. Pass "EPSG:4326" SQL already returns WGS84 geometry, source CRS string (example "EPSG:4267") DuckDB reproject WGS84 tiling. file-based input CRS embedded file, use freestile_file() engine = "duckdb" instead, auto-detects source CRS.

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Query a GeoParquet file freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('data.parquet') WHERE pop > 50000",   "output.pmtiles" )  # Query a Shapefile freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM ST_Read('counties.shp')",   "counties.pmtiles" )  # Query with an existing DuckDB database freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM my_table WHERE region = 'West'",   "west.pmtiles",   db_path = "my_database.duckdb" ) } # }

what: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
title: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
text: Create vector tile archives 'PMTiles' format 'sf' spatial data frames. Supports 'Mapbox Vector Tile' ('MVT') 'MapLibre Tile' ('MLT') output formats. Uses 'Rust' backend via 'extendr' fast, -memory tiling zero external system dependencies.

title: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
text: Maintainer: Kyle Walker kyle@walker-data.com

what: View an H3 hexagonal-binning PMTiles archive — view_h3_tiles
title: View an H3 hexagonal-binning PMTiles archive — view_h3_tiles
text: Reads metadata PMTiles archive produced freestile_h3() builds mapgl map one fill layer per H3 resolution plus circle layer raw points. Automatically detects whether archive built fade = FALSE (disjoint zoom windows, clean breaks) fade = TRUE (overlapping windows, cross-fade) styles accordingly.

title: View an H3 hexagonal-binning PMTiles archive — view_h3_tiles
code: view_h3_tiles(   input,   agg_column = NULL,   stops = NULL,   palette = "viridis",   hex_opacity = 0.9,   point_color = "#0868ac",   point_radius = NULL,   background_style = NULL,   hex_layer_prefix = "h3",   point_layer_name = "points",   port = 8080 )

title: View an H3 hexagonal-binning PMTiles archive — view_h3_tiles
text: input Path local .pmtiles file produced freestile_h3(). agg_column Character NULL. Aggregation column drive hex color scale. NULL, first non-h3_id numeric field metadata used. stops List values colors (equal length, sorted values) defining shared color scale across hex layers. NULL, documented quick-look default used. palette Character. Named palette used generate default colors stops NULL. Currently supports "viridis" (default), "magma", "plasma", "cividis", "inferno", "rocket", "mako", "turbo" viridisLite installed; otherwise falls back five-color blue ramp. hex_opacity Numeric. Peak fill opacity hex layers (default 0.9). fade mode opacity layer's center zoom. point_color Character. Color raw-point circles (default "#0868ac"). point_radius mapgl expression NULL. NULL, zoom-interpolated default used. background_style mapgl style passed mapgl::maplibre(); NULL uses mapgl::maplibre() defaults. hex_layer_prefix Character. Prefix used archive written. Must match value passed freestile_h3(); default "h3". point_layer_name Character. Name raw-points MVT layer archive. Must match value passed freestile_h3(); default "points". port Integer. Port local PMTiles server (default 8080).

title: View an H3 hexagonal-binning PMTiles archive — view_h3_tiles
text: mapgl map object.

title: View an H3 hexagonal-binning PMTiles archive — view_h3_tiles
text: default color scale documented quick-look ramp (5 evenly spaced breaks across 1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000). production maps, pass explicit stops = list(values = ..., colors = ...) derived data. Embedding real aggregation statistics PMTiles metadata roadmap.

title: View an H3 hexagonal-binning PMTiles archive — view_h3_tiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ view_h3_tiles("wind.pmtiles", agg_column = "n",   stops = list(values = c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000),                colors = viridisLite::viridis(5))) } # }

what: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
text: Starts local tile server (needed) creates interactive mapgl map showing tileset. Layer type styling auto-detected PMTiles metadata possible.

title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
code: view_tiles(   input,   layer = NULL,   layer_type = NULL,   color = NULL,   opacity = 0.5,   port = 8080,   promote_id = NULL )

title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
text: input Path local .pmtiles file. layer Character. Source layer name display. NULL (default), first layer tileset used. layer_type Character. Map layer type: "fill", "line", "circle". NULL, auto-detected PMTiles metadata geometry type. color Fill, line, circle color. Default "navy" fill line layers, "steelblue" circle layers. opacity Numeric opacity (0–1). Default 0.5. port Port local tile server. Default 8080. promote_id Character. Property name use feature ID hover interactivity. NULL, feature promotion used.

title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
text: mapgl map object (can piped mapgl operations).

title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ freestile(nc, "nc.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") view_tiles("nc.pmtiles")  # Override auto-detection view_tiles("roads.pmtiles", layer_type = "line", color = "red")  # Point data view_tiles("airports.pmtiles", layer_type = "circle", color = "orange") } # }

title: freestiler 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-24 Thin (sub-pixel-width) polygons longer flicker across zoom levels (#13). polygon collapses tile’s integer pixel grid now replaced one-pixel square centroid instead dropped, narrow features stay continuously visible. Ring winding also normalized MVT specification (exterior rings positive area, interior rings negative), zero-area degenerate rings dropped rather emitted invalid polygons, quantization-induced spikes (--back needle vertices) removed. freestile_h3() new function dynamic hexagonal binning. aggregates points H3 hexagons zoom-appropriate resolutions via DuckDB’s H3 community extension writes multi-layer .pmtiles archive low zooms show coarse hexes, intermediate zooms show progressively finer hexes, zooms base_zoom show raw points. Aggregation rules user-defined SQL expressions (e.g. c(n = "COUNT(*)", avg_pop = "AVG(pop)")). Opt-fade = TRUE produces overlapping zoom windows adjacent hex resolutions can cross-fade visually. view_h3_tiles() companion viewer auto-styles freestile_h3() archive mapgl, detecting clean-break vs cross-fade mode PMTiles metadata. Hexagons cross antimeridian split +/-180 degrees rather rendering world-spanning slivers. See vignette("h3-hexagonal-binning") walkthrough.

title: freestiler 0.1.7
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-12 Updated CRAN Rust build path use dependency graph compatible rustc/cargo 1.77.2.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: freestile() creates PMTiles archives sf data frames zero external dependencies (tippecanoe, Java, Go). Supports MapLibre Tiles (MLT) Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT) output formats. Multi-layer output via named lists freestile_layer() per-layer zoom control.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: POINT, MULTIPOINT, LINESTRING, MULTILINESTRING, POLYGON, MULTIPOLYGON. Automatic CRS transformation WGS84. Z/M dimension handling (dropped automatically).

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Parallel tile encoding rayon (across tiles within tiles). Tile-pixel grid snapping zoom-adaptive simplification without slivers. Buffered tile assignment clipping seamless tile boundaries.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: drop_rate exponential feature thinning Morton-curve spatial ordering points area-based ordering polygons/lines. base_zoom control ensuring features present higher zooms. cluster_distance point clustering point_count attribute. coalesce line merging polygon grouping.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Spec-compliant MapLibre Tile encoder varint, delta, RLE, dictionary encoding. Validated mlt-core 0.1.2 reference decoder.
